creator: marino@FreeBSD.org
text:
Peter, as maintainer, you need to approve or disapprove this patch.  If you disapprove it, you need to say what should be modified to achieve approval or state why it will never will.

It's common for maintainers to push changes upstream as well, not just push that off to  people that write PRs.  Often maintainers have a better knowledge/relationship with the upstream that the submitter does and would be more successful at getting fixes in.

So in general comment#2 isn't valid.  the "maintainer" has a lot of responsibility.
